message.content:
@startuml
skinparam packageStyle rectangle

package "tictactoe" {
    folder controllers <<Folder>> {
        class Controller {
            +Controller(Game) #
            -getToken(Coordinate): Token $green$
        }
        
        class StartController {
            +StartController(Game) $green$
            -setUsers(int) $green$
            getMaxPlayers(): int $green$
        }
        
        class ResumeController {
            +ResumeController(Game) $green$
            resume() $green$
        }
        
        class PlayController {
            +PlayController(Game) $green$
            isBoardComplete(): boolean $green$
            isTicTacToe(): boolean $green$
            getToken(): Token $green$
            isUser(): boolean $green$
            put(Coordinate): Error $green$
            move(Coordinate,Coordinate): Error $green$
        }
        
        class Logic {
            +Logic() $green$
            getMaxPlayers(): int $green$
            setUsers(int) $green$
            getToken(): Token $green$
            next() $green$
            isBoardComplete(): boolean $green$
            isTicTacToe(): boolean $green$
            isUser(): boolean $green$
            put(Coordinate): Error $green$
            move(Coordinate, Coordinate): Error $green$
            getToken(Coordinate): Token $green$
            resume() $green$
        }
    }
    
    usantatecla.tictactoe.models.Game as Game
    usantatecla.tictactoe.types.Token as Token
    
}

usantatecla.tictactoe.models.Coordinate as Coordinate
usantatecla.tictactoe.types.Error as Error


' Relationships from Controllers to Models/Types
Game <|-- Controller : extends 
Token ..> Controller 

Controllers --> Game  
controllers .u.>  coordinate  

controller --* logic   
logic *-- playcontroller   

playcontroller o--> game     
game <-- startcontroller      
resumecontroller -> gamemodels.game    

note right of controller::gettoken(coordinate)\nreturns token\nbased on input\ncorresponding position.\nnote leftof logics methods list contains all necessary operations required during gameplay including checking win conditions etcetera..



@enduml
